---
title: "Hardware, Embedded, and Performance-Near Words"
---

# Hardware, Embedded, and Performance-Near Words

Range: `1211-1290`.

## Completion

| Status   | Count |
| -------- | ----- |
| authored | 15    |
| stub     | 65    |

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                                           | Completion | Summary                                                                                                                                           |
| ----- | ---------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1211  | `STUB` [accelerator](#rune-1211)               | stub       | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1212  | `STUB` [alignment {HW}](#rune-1212)            | stub       | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1213  | `STUB` [ASIC](#rune-1213)                      | stub       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1214  | `STUB` [assembly instruction](#rune-1214)      | stub       | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1215  | [atomic instruction](#rune-1215)               | authored   | An indivisible machine operation at instruction level.                                                                                            |
| 1216  | `STUB` [bandwidth {HW}](#rune-1216)            | stub       | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1217  | `STUB` [barrier {HW}](#rune-1217)              | stub       | A rendezvous word; no party crosses until all required parties arrive.                                                                            |
| 1218  | `STUB` [bit width](#rune-1218)                 | stub       | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1219  | `STUB` [boot ROM](#rune-1219)                  | stub       | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1220  | `STUB` [branch predictor](#rune-1220)          | stub       | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1221  | `STUB` [bus {HW}](#rune-1221)                  | stub       | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1222  | [cache {HW}](#rune-1222)                       | authored   | A nearness word; it keeps likely things close to reduce future cost.                                                                              |
| 1223  | `STUB` [cache coherence](#rune-1223)           | stub       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1224  | [cache line {HW}](#rune-1224)                  | authored   | The coherence-sized chunk of cached memory; too much sharing here hurts.                                                                          |
| 1225  | `STUB` [clock](#rune-1225)                     | stub       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1226  | `STUB` [clock cycle](#rune-1226)               | stub       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1227  | `STUB` [co-processor](#rune-1227)              | stub       | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1228  | `STUB` [core {HW}](#rune-1228)                 | stub       | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1229  | `STUB` [CPU](#rune-1229)                       | stub       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1230  | `STUB` [cycle budget](#rune-1230)              | stub       | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1231  | [DMA](#rune-1231)                              | authored   | Direct memory access: device moves bytes without making the CPU do every step.                                                                    |
| 1232  | `STUB` [DRAM](#rune-1232)                      | stub       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1233  | `STUB` [driver interrupt](#rune-1233)          | stub       | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1234  | `STUB` [EEPROM](#rune-1234)                    | stub       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1235  | [endianness](#rune-1235)                       | authored   | The byte order in which multi-byte values are laid out.                                                                                           |
| 1236  | `STUB` [fence](#rune-1236)                     | stub       | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1237  | [firmware {HW}](#rune-1237)                    | authored   | Software that is closer to the board than the application.                                                                                        |
| 1238  | `STUB` [flash](#rune-1238)                     | stub       | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1239  | `STUB` [FPGA](#rune-1239)                      | stub       | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1240  | `STUB` [frame budget](#rune-1240)              | stub       | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1241  | `STUB` [frequency](#rune-1241)                 | stub       | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1242  | `STUB` [GPIO](#rune-1242)                      | stub       | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1243  | [GPU](#rune-1243)                              | authored   | A massively parallel processor built for throughput-heavy workloads.                                                                              |
| 1244  | `STUB` [heat budget](#rune-1244)               | stub       | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1245  | `STUB` [I2C](#rune-1245)                       | stub       | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1246  | `STUB` [instruction cache](#rune-1246)         | stub       | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1247  | `STUB` [instruction pointer](#rune-1247)       | stub       | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1248  | `STUB` [interrupt service routine](#rune-1248) | stub       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1249  | `STUB` [ioctl boundary](#rune-1249)            | stub       | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1250  | `STUB` [IPC cost](#rune-1250)                  | stub       | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1251  | [ISA](#rune-1251)                              | authored   | Instruction set architecture: the machine contract visible to software.                                                                           |
| 1252  | `STUB` [jitter {HW}](#rune-1252)               | stub       | A timing-noise word; repeated operations arrive with unstable spacing.                                                                            |
| 1253  | `STUB` [JTAG](#rune-1253)                      | stub       | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1254  | `STUB` [kernel bypass](#rune-1254)             | stub       | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1255  | [latency budget](#rune-1255)                   | authored   | The maximum delay the system is allowed to spend and still remain acceptable.                                                                     |
| 1256  | `STUB` [memory channel](#rune-1256)            | stub       | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1257  | `STUB` [memory-mapped I/O](#rune-1257)         | stub       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1258  | `STUB` [microcode](#rune-1258)                 | stub       | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1259  | `STUB` [MMIO](#rune-1259)                      | stub       | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1260  | `STUB` [NUMA](#rune-1260)                      | stub       | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1261  | `STUB` [NVRAM](#rune-1261)                     | stub       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1262  | `STUB` [opcode {HW}](#rune-1262)               | stub       | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1263  | `STUB` [out-of-order execution](#rune-1263)    | stub       | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1264  | `STUB` [PCIe](#rune-1264)                      | stub       | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1265  | `STUB` [pipeline {HW}](#rune-1265)             | stub       | A procession word; work passes through ordered stages.                                                                                            |
| 1266  | `STUB` [power budget](#rune-1266)              | stub       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1267  | `STUB` [prefetcher](#rune-1267)                | stub       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1268  | `STUB` [real-time {HW}](#rune-1268)            | stub       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1269  | [register](#rune-1269)                         | authored   | A tiny named storage cell inside the processor.                                                                                                   |
| 1270  | `STUB` [register file](#rune-1270)             | stub       | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1271  | `STUB` [reset vector](#rune-1271)              | stub       | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1272  | `STUB` [ROM](#rune-1272)                       | stub       | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1273  | `STUB` [RTOS](#rune-1273)                      | stub       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1274  | `STUB` [runtime budget](#rune-1274)            | stub       | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1275  | `STUB` [sensor loop](#rune-1275)               | stub       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1276  | [SIMD](#rune-1276)                             | authored   | One instruction operating across many data lanes at once.                                                                                         |
| 1277  | `STUB` [SPI](#rune-1277)                       | stub       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1278  | `STUB` [SRAM](#rune-1278)                      | stub       | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1279  | `STUB` [superscalar](#rune-1279)               | stub       | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1280  | `STUB` [thermal throttling](#rune-1280)        | stub       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1281  | [throughput](#rune-1281)                       | authored   | How much useful work the system can push through a narrow world in bounded time. Shadow: chasing bulk capacity while tail latency burns the user. |
| 1282  | `STUB` [timing window](#rune-1282)             | stub       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1283  | `STUB` [TPU](#rune-1283)                       | stub       | A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.                                                                         |
| 1284  | `STUB` [UART](#rune-1284)                      | stub       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1285  | [utilization](#rune-1285)                      | authored   | The fraction of available capacity actually being used.                                                                                           |
| 1286  | `STUB` [vector unit](#rune-1286)               | stub       | An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.                                                                      |
| 1287  | [volatile](#rune-1287)                         | authored   | A promise to the compiler that this value may change through unusual means.                                                                       |
| 1288  | `STUB` [watchdog timer](#rune-1288)            | stub       | A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.                                                         |
| 1289  | `STUB` [wire speed](#rune-1289)                | stub       | A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.                                                                                |
| 1290  | [zero-copy {HW}](#rune-1290)                   | authored   | Near-metal data movement without redundant duplication.                                                                                           |

## Entries

### [1211] accelerator {#rune-1211}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1212] alignment {HW} {#rune-1212}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1213] ASIC {#rune-1213}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1214] assembly instruction {#rune-1214}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1215] atomic instruction {#rune-1215}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** An indivisible machine operation at instruction level.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

**Pocket gloss:** An indivisible machine operation at instruction level.

### [1216] bandwidth {HW} {#rune-1216}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1217] barrier {HW} {#rune-1217}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A rendezvous word; no party crosses until all required parties arrive.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1218] bit width {#rune-1218}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1219] boot ROM {#rune-1219}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1220] branch predictor {#rune-1220}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1221] bus {HW} {#rune-1221}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1222] cache {HW} {#rune-1222}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A nearness word; it keeps likely things close to reduce future cost.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1223] cache coherence {#rune-1223}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1224] cache line {HW} {#rune-1224}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The coherence-sized chunk of cached memory; too much sharing here hurts.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

**Pocket gloss:** The coherence-sized chunk of cached memory; too much sharing here hurts.

### [1225] clock {#rune-1225}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1226] clock cycle {#rune-1226}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1227] co-processor {#rune-1227}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1228] core {HW} {#rune-1228}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1229] CPU {#rune-1229}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1230] cycle budget {#rune-1230}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1231] DMA {#rune-1231}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Direct memory access: device moves bytes without making the CPU do every step.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

**Pocket gloss:** Direct memory access: device moves bytes without making the CPU do every step.

### [1232] DRAM {#rune-1232}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1233] driver interrupt {#rune-1233}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1234] EEPROM {#rune-1234}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1235] endianness {#rune-1235}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The byte order in which multi-byte values are laid out.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

**Pocket gloss:** The byte order in which multi-byte values are laid out.

### [1236] fence {#rune-1236}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1237] firmware {HW} {#rune-1237}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Software that is closer to the board than the application.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

**Pocket gloss:** Software that is closer to the board than the application.

### [1238] flash {#rune-1238}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1239] FPGA {#rune-1239}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1240] frame budget {#rune-1240}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1241] frequency {#rune-1241}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1242] GPIO {#rune-1242}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1243] GPU {#rune-1243}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A massively parallel processor built for throughput-heavy workloads.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

**Pocket gloss:** A massively parallel processor built for throughput-heavy workloads.

### [1244] heat budget {#rune-1244}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1245] I2C {#rune-1245}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1246] instruction cache {#rune-1246}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1247] instruction pointer {#rune-1247}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1248] interrupt service routine {#rune-1248}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1249] ioctl boundary {#rune-1249}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1250] IPC cost {#rune-1250}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1251] ISA {#rune-1251}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Instruction set architecture: the machine contract visible to software.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

**Pocket gloss:** Instruction set architecture: the machine contract visible to software.

### [1252] jitter {HW} {#rune-1252}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A timing-noise word; repeated operations arrive with unstable spacing.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1253] JTAG {#rune-1253}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1254] kernel bypass {#rune-1254}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1255] latency budget {#rune-1255}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The maximum delay the system is allowed to spend and still remain acceptable.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

**Pocket gloss:** The maximum delay the system is allowed to spend and still remain acceptable.

### [1256] memory channel {#rune-1256}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1257] memory-mapped I/O {#rune-1257}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1258] microcode {#rune-1258}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1259] MMIO {#rune-1259}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1260] NUMA {#rune-1260}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1261] NVRAM {#rune-1261}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1262] opcode {HW} {#rune-1262}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1263] out-of-order execution {#rune-1263}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1264] PCIe {#rune-1264}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1265] pipeline {HW} {#rune-1265}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A procession word; work passes through ordered stages.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1266] power budget {#rune-1266}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1267] prefetcher {#rune-1267}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1268] real-time {HW} {#rune-1268}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1269] register {#rune-1269}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A tiny named storage cell inside the processor.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

**Pocket gloss:** A tiny named storage cell inside the processor.

### [1270] register file {#rune-1270}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1271] reset vector {#rune-1271}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1272] ROM {#rune-1272}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1273] RTOS {#rune-1273}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1274] runtime budget {#rune-1274}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1275] sensor loop {#rune-1275}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1276] SIMD {#rune-1276}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** One instruction operating across many data lanes at once.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

**Pocket gloss:** One instruction operating across many data lanes at once.

### [1277] SPI {#rune-1277}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1278] SRAM {#rune-1278}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1279] superscalar {#rune-1279}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1280] thermal throttling {#rune-1280}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1281] throughput {#rune-1281}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** How much useful work the system can push through a narrow world in bounded time.

**Shadow:** chasing bulk capacity while tail latency burns the user.

**Pocket gloss:** How much useful work passes through in bounded time.

### [1282] timing window {#rune-1282}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1283] TPU {#rune-1283}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A near-metal word; it matters when abstraction is no longer cheap enough.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1284] UART {#rune-1284}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1285] utilization {#rune-1285}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** The fraction of available capacity actually being used.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

**Pocket gloss:** The fraction of available capacity actually being used.

### [1286] vector unit {#rune-1286}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** An embodiment word; software here speaks almost directly to silicon or wire.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1287] volatile {#rune-1287}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** A promise to the compiler that this value may change through unusual means.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

**Pocket gloss:** A promise to the compiler that this value may change through unusual means.

### [1288] watchdog timer {#rune-1288}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A performance-and-physics word; it governs timing, placement, and finite material limits.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1289] wire speed {#rune-1289}

**Status:** canonical

**Completion:** stub

**Force:** A machine-word; it reaches into clocks, buses, registers, or heat.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

### [1290] zero-copy {HW} {#rune-1290}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Completion:** authored

**Force:** Near-metal data movement without redundant duplication.

**Shadow:** physical constraints can turn clean software assumptions into timing, heat, or bandwidth failures.

**Pocket gloss:** Near-metal data movement without redundant duplication.
